evals.expected_output: A structured code review with security issues flagged (hardcoded secret, IP spoofing via x-forwarded-for, req.body.email before parsing), verdict, and actionable suggestions with code examples

assertions.name: flags_hardcoded_secret
assertions.description: Flags 'secret123' hardcoded in skip condition as a security issue

assertions.name: flags_ip_spoofing
assertions.description: Notes that x-forwarded-for can be spoofed by clients

assertions.name: has_verdict
assertions.description: Includes a clear verdict (approve / request changes)

assertions.name: has_structured_output
assertions.description: Output is organized with sections, not a wall of prose

evals.prompt: Help me write my standup. Yesterday I debugged a production outage — the API was returning 502s because nginx was passing a wrong Connection header to Fastify which broke the keepalive pool. Fixed it with a sed on the nginx config and a docker exec to reload. Also reviewed a PR for rate limiting. Today I'm working on integrating Stripe for the Pro tier and finishing up the Pinecone semantic search task. No blockers.
evals.expected_output: A clean, structured standup in yesterday/today/blockers format. Should be concise, action-oriented, and not just parrot back the raw notes verbatim.

assertions.name: has_three_sections
assertions.description: Output contains Yesterday, Today, and Blockers sections

assertions.name: summarizes_not_dumps
assertions.description: Output is more concise than the input — doesn't just repeat all the technical detail verbatim

assertions.name: has_date_or_header
assertions.description: Output has a date or standup header

evals.prompt: I need to design the semantic search feature for WorldPulse. Users should be able to type a natural language query like 'earthquake in New Zealand' and find relevant signals even if the exact words don't match. We're already using PostgreSQL and Redis. The API is Node.js/Fastify. We have maybe 2000 signals right now but could grow to 100k+. Budget is tight — we want to use Pinecone's free tier to start.
evals.expected_output: A practical system design with component diagram, data model, API design, trade-off analysis, and a realistic v1 vs future-state distinction

assertions.name: covers_embedding_strategy
assertions.description: Addresses how signals get embedded (what model, when, batch vs real-time)

assertions.name: addresses_sync
assertions.description: Mentions how to keep Pinecone in sync with new signals

assertions.name: mentions_v1_scoping
assertions.description: Distinguishes what to build first vs what to do later

evals.prompt: Do a tech debt audit on our API codebase. The main issues I know about: we have two DB containers running (wp_db and wp_postgres) and nobody's sure which is canonical. The scraper has been running for 5 days and we've never looked at its error logs. Some routes use raw SQL, some use Knex. Redis cache keys are inconsistent across files — some use colons as separators, some use hyphens. We don't have any integration tests, just unit tests for a few utility functions.
evals.expected_output: A structured tech debt audit with categorized findings, priority scores, quick wins, and a remediation plan. Should frame the business risk, not just the technical issue.

assertions.name: uses_categories
assertions.description: Categorizes debt items (code debt, infra debt, test debt, etc.)

assertions.name: has_prioritization
assertions.description: Provides some form of priority ranking or scoring

assertions.name: flags_dual_db
assertions.description: Flags the dual-database situation as a risk

assertions.name: has_remediation_plan
assertions.description: Includes concrete next steps or a phased plan
